skill.name: tdd-enforcer
skill.description: Enforces a test-driven development workflow for new features, bug fixes, and refactors by requiring tests before implementation.
skill.summary: Guides agents through red, green, and refactor development with clear test quality checks.
skill.author: Barnhardt-Enterprises-Inc

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk strings, but review shows they are documentation examples for tests, passwords, routes, and coverage commands. The real risk is moderate because the skill allows Bash and directs agents to run project test commands; no malicious intent, exfiltration, or prompt injection was found.

medium_findings.title: Bash-Enabled Test Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill permits Bash and instructs the agent to run npm test and coverage commands. This is expected for a TDD workflow, but it can execute arbitrary project scripts in the user workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Bash permission and npm commands are explicit. The commands are standard testing commands, so this is a controlled workflow risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Documentation-Only Network URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are localhost API route examples used inside integration test documentation. They do not send data to an external service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both URLs point to localhost and appear inside test example code. No external endpoint or credential transfer is present.

low_findings.title: Documentation-Only Relative Import Path
low_findings.description: The path traversal alert is a relative import for a Prisma mock inside a test example. It is not file access or path construction from user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is an import statement in a TypeScript test example. There is no dynamic path handling or filesystem operation.

low_findings.title: Security Keywords In Test Examples
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts are triggered by words such as password, bcrypt, invalid id, and invalid JSON in testing examples. No weak algorithm or system probing behavior is implemented by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is embedded in illustrative tests and checklist items. The skill contains no executable crypto implementation, scanner, or reconnaissance logic.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill tells the agent to run npm test and npm coverage commands. Users should review package scripts before running them in untrusted repositories.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command instructions are explicit and relevant to the workflow. Risk depends on the repository package scripts, not on hidden behavior in the skill.

content.value_statement: Teams often add code before proving expected behavior with tests. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through red, green, and refactor steps.
actual_capabilities: Requires tests before implementation for features, fixes, and refactors.
Defines red, green, and refactor workflow checkpoints.
Promotes Arrange, Act, Assert structure for readable tests.
Sets coverage targets for business logic, utilities, UI, and critical flows.
Provides examples for unit, integration, component, and end-to-end tests.
limitations: It does not choose the correct test runner for every project automatically.
It cannot guarantee coverage unless project tooling measures it.
It may run package scripts that contain project-defined behavior.
It provides guidance and examples, not a full testing framework.

use_cases.title: Add a Feature With Tests First
use_cases.description: Define failing tests for the new behavior, then implement the smallest code needed to pass them.

use_cases.title: Fix a Bug With Regression Coverage
use_cases.description: Capture the bug with a failing test before changing production code.

use_cases.title: Review Test Quality During Refactors
use_cases.description: Check that tests cover behavior, edge cases, errors, and critical flows before restructuring code.

prompt_templates.title: Start a TDD Feature
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for a small feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the TDD enforcer workflow for this feature. First write focused failing tests, then wait for confirmation before implementing.

prompt_templates.title: Add Regression Coverage
prompt_templates.scenario: Bug fix with a clear failing case.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to reproduce the bug with a failing test. Explain the failure reason before writing the fix.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Test Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these tests using the skill guidance. Improve names, AAA structure, edge cases, and error coverage without changing behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Full Coverage Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for a larger feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a TDD plan across unit, integration, component, and end-to-end tests. Include coverage goals and validation commands.

output_examples.output: A red phase plan with tests for valid registration, invalid email, weak passwords, and hidden password fields.

output_examples.input: Request to fix failed login errors.
output_examples.output: A regression test that proves the error state, followed by a minimal implementation path and validation step.

output_examples.input: Request to refactor an authentication service.
output_examples.output: A checklist confirming tests stay green, behavior remains covered, and implementation details are not over-tested.

best_practices: Review test commands before running them in untrusted repositories.
Keep tests focused on observable behavior and user-visible outcomes.
Confirm the first failure proves the intended missing behavior.
anti_patterns: Writing implementation code before the first failing test exists.
Changing tests only to match an incorrect implementation.
Testing private implementation details instead of behavior.

faq.question: Does this skill write tests before code?
faq.answer: Yes. It directs the assistant to create failing tests before implementation work starts.

faq.question: Can it run my test suite?
faq.answer: Yes, it can ask Bash to run test commands such as npm test when the environment allows it.

faq.question: Is it limited to JavaScript projects?
faq.answer: The examples use TypeScript and npm, but the TDD process can guide other languages with adapted commands.

faq.question: Does it guarantee high coverage?
faq.answer: No. It defines coverage targets, but your project tooling must measure and enforce them.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill allows Bash and test commands. That is useful, but project scripts can execute code in the workspace.

faq.question: Does the audit show malicious behavior?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed content shows testing guidance and examples, with no exfiltration or prompt injection evidence.
